steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $11x+4y$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Group the $x$ terms together: $6x + 5x = 11x$. The $y$ term has no like terms, so it stays as $4y$. Result: $11x + 4y$.

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content: The simplified expression is $11x + 4y$. That matches Choice C — $11x+4y$.

choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — $15x$: Adds all three terms as if they were like terms and drops $4y$ entirely — $x$ and $y$ terms cannot be combined. ✗
  • Choice B — $15y$: Treats all terms as $y$ terms and uses the wrong sum — no basis for this. ✗
  • Choice C — $11x+4y$: $6x + 5x = 11x$ and $4y$ remains unchanged. ✓
  • Choice D — $30x+4y$: Multiplies the $x$ coefficients (6 × 5 = 30) instead of adding them. ✗
That matches Choice C — $11x+4y$.
